student_message: I folded it! The two sides match perfectly.
final_message:
That is wonderful! Since the two sides match perfectly, that fold line right down the middle has a special name. It is called a line of symmetry. A line of symmetry is just a fold line that divides a shape into two halves that are perfect mirror images of each other. 

Now, let's put on our secret agent glasses and look closely! 🕵️‍♂️ If you fold the uppercase letter A right down the middle, both halves match perfectly, just like your heart. That means it has one line of symmetry. Now, picture the big uppercase letter H in your mind. Can you figure out how many hidden lines of symmetry it has? ✨

orchestrator_intent.goal: Define the fold as the line of symmetry and regain her visual attention.
orchestrator_intent.reasoning: The student understands the concept of matching sides but is looking away. Dialogue will teach the term 'line of symmetry' while engagement brings her focus back.

current_section_title: Reflection Symmetry and Lines of Symmetry
current_section_content: A line of symmetry is a fold line that divides a shape into two halves that are perfect mirror images of each other. Some shapes have a vertical line of symmetry (left-right mirror), some have a horizontal line (top-bottom mirror), and some have both. You can use this idea to cut out shapes by drawing only half or one-quarter of the shape on folded paper, then cutting and unfolding. Letters like A have one vertical line of symmetry, while letters like H have both vertical and horizontal lines of symmetry.
